Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BAMDAD BEHNOOD Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Bamdad Behnood's motion to strike or tax costs claimed by Plaintiffs and Cross-Defendants Dental Force, Inc. and Richard Evangelista is denied in light of Cross-Defendants' reduction of their cost claim to $6,922.52.

On October 26, 2020, Dental Force and Evangelista filed a memorandum of costs seeking to recover $7,768.02 in costs, including $2,000 in filing and motion fees, $150 in jury fees, $3,579.16 in deposition costs, $788.51 for costs of service of process, $60 in witness fees, and $1190.35 in fees for electronic filing or service. The memo of costs does not seek costs incurred on behalf of Plaintiff Evangelista Dental Group SF 450 Sutter, PC. In response to Defendant's motion, Dental Force and Evangelista have corrected the amount of costs they are seeking, reducing the total to $6,922.52, including $1,490 in filing fees, $3,579.16 in deposition costs, $788.51 in costs for service of process, $60 in witness fees, and $1,004.85 in costs of electronic filing and service.

On November 21, 2019, pursuant to an accepted Code of Civil Procedure section 998 offer, Plaintiff Dental Force dismissed the First Amended Complaint against Behnood with prejudice. By the offer, Defendant offered to settle Dental Force's claims against her for a waiver of her right to recover costs and attorney fees. The offer stated, "This offer includes Plaintiff DENTAL FORCE, INC.'s proportionate share of recoverable costs and attorney fees, if any," and also stated, "Each party to bear their own respective costs, expenses, and attorneys' fees." (Miller Decl. par. 7 & Exs. A, B.) On August 17, 2020, Plaintiff Evangelista Dental Group SF 450 Sutter, PC filed a dismissal of the complaint against Defendant without prejudice. (par. 8 & Ex. C.) On September 18, 2020, two days before the assigned trial date, Behnood dismissed her cross-complaint without prejudice. (id. par. 9 & Ex. D.)

Defendant's argument that Dental Force waived the right to recover all costs by accepting her section 998 offer is rejected. That offer, by its terms, extended only to Dental Force's claims against Defendant, but preserved her claims against it in the cross-complaint, which she did not dismiss until nearly one year later. By virtue of that dismissal, Dental Force and Evangelista were prevailing parties on the cross-complaint. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1032(a)(4) ["prevailing party" includes a party in whose favor a dismissal is entered].) As such, they are entitled to recover their costs incurred in connection with that pleading as a matter of right. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1032(b).) Plaintiffs show that the costs they seek to recover were incurred in connection with Defendant's claims against them, and have voluntarily removed electronic filing fees incurred before Defendant's motion for leave to file the cross-complaint against them. (Ficenec Decl., pars. 7, 8, 10.)

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
James Ficenec (James.Ficenec@ndlf.com) for Plaintiffs.
Davis Reilly (dreilly@bledsoelaw.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs DENTAL FORCE, INC., EVANGELISTA DENTAL GROUP SAN FRANCISCO 450 SUTTER's Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs:

Dental Force, Inc., Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, PC (EDG) and Richard Evangelista move to strike or tax costs. These parties were in litigation - now dismissed - with Bemdad Behnood. Behnood filed a costs memorandum, later clarifying that costs were only sought from EDG. EDG moved to strike or tax costs regarding depositions of Behnood, Evangelista and a PMK and expert fees of Eric Drabkin and Kevin Clarke. (Not. 2:9-15.)

Behnood made a $7,500 CCP 998 offer to EDG in October 2019 that was not accepted. In August 2020, EDG dismissed its claims against Behnood without prejudice, making her a prevailing party who obtained a more favorable result than the 998 offer. (Cano v. Glover (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 326, 331.)

EDG moved in limine to exclude expert Drabkin, but does not claim the motion was ever ruled upon. EDG represents that Clarke "was never identified as an expert anywhere," but he in fact was. (Ex. A to Ficenic Dec. Ex. G.)

The depositions regarded Dental Force, Evangelista, Behnood and EDG's interrelated claims. EDG argues that "the court must apportion the costs to be assessed," relying on Hepler v. J.M. Peters Co., Inc. (1993) 73 Cal.App.4th 1265. However, in Hepler the judge who awarded costs had presided over trial. Here, EDG would have had me shoot in the dark - there was no trial and EDG proffered no analytical tools to enable an apportionment.

Given the above, I gave the parties an opportunity to file supplemental briefs on apportionment of costs. I now further rule as follows:

Behnood's costs memo requested $31,834 (all amounts rounded to nearest dollar). Behnood conceded that Item 4.d. ($1,211) should be stricken, yielding a new total of $30,623. Costs associated with the depositions of Behnood, Evangelista and the PMK, as well as Drabkin's expert fees, are taxed by two-thirds so EDG pays a pro rata share of them. The total costs award from EDG to Behnood is thus $17,835.

Counsel for Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Defendant Bamdad Behnoods Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Confidential Records Submitted In Support Of Opposition To Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Adjudication Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT BAMDAD BEHNOOD'S Motion To File Confidential Records Submitted In Support Of Opposition To Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Adjudication Under Seal.

Defendant Bamdad Behnood's motion for a sealing order is denied without prejudice. Behnood moves to seal 16 documents related to a summary adjudication motion in their entirety. However, Behnood fails to make the showings that would allow the court to enter the express factual findings required. (CRC 2.550(d).) Two examples: CRC 2.550(d) requires showings of "an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record" and that the "proposed sealing is narrowly tailored." Labelling something "confidential" or "highly confidential" under a protective order does not relieve a party of its obligations under CRC 2.550 and 2.551.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar Aug-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS DENTAL FORCE, INC., EVANGELISTA DENTAL GROUP SAN FRANCISCO 450 SUTTER, AND RICHARD'S Motion (1) To Bifurcate The Cross-Complaint Of Defendant And Cross-Complaint Bamdad Benhood;(2) Set A Separate Trial Date As To The Cross-Complaint; (3) Have Discovery As To The Cross-Complaint Proceed On A Separate Track; And (4) Have The Trial Date As To The Cross-Complaint Be Deemed The Intial Trial Date As To That Pleading Only.

APPEARANCES: Atty. MARIA NOZZOLINO, ESQ. of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane LLP (Tel: 415-981-5411) appearing for defendant Bemdad Behnood;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion by plaintiffs and cross-defendants Dental Force, Inc. and Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, POC and cross-defendant Richard Evangelista (1) to bifurcate the cross-complaint; (2) set a separate trial date as to the cross-complaint; (3) have discovery as to the cross-complaint proceed on a separate track; and (4) have the trial date as to the cross-complaint be deemed the initial trial date as to that pleading only is denied. There are likely to be some common witnesses, and it would serve judicial economy for the entire case to be resolved in one trial, rather than two separate trials. If the moving parties need additional time to complete discovery or to move for summary judgment on the cross-complaint, they may seek to continue the trial date. This ruling is without prejudice to the parties requesting the trial judge to bifurcate the cross-complaint or certain issues for trial. (See Grappo v. Coventry Financial Corp. (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 496, 504 ["trial courts have broad discretion to determine the order of proof in the interests of judicial economy"].)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Off Calendar May-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BAMDAD BEHNOOD'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

Defendant Bemdad Behnood's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. Even if the cross-complaint is not compulsory as to Plaintiff Dental Force, Inc., the motion is supported by California's policy of liberality in pleading and by the interest in judicial economy. (Code Civ. Proc. 428.50(c); see City of Hanford v. Superior Court (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 580, 587 ["The reason for allowing cross-complaints is to have a complete determination of a controversy among the parties in one action, thus avoiding circuity of action and duplication of time and effort."].) Defendant has not delayed unduly in seeking leave to file her cross-complaint, and Plaintiffs have not shown that they would be prejudiced if the Court were to grant the motion. Plaintiffs may raise any objections they have to the allegations in the proposed cross-complaint on summary judgment.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

PLAINTIFFS DENTAL FORCE, INC., EVANGELISTA DENTAL GROUP SAN FRANCISCO 450 SUTTER'S Motion To Recover Attorneys Fees And Costs In The Amount Of At Least $12,899.67 From Defendant Bemdad Behnood.

Plaintiffs Dental Force, Inc. and Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, PC's motion to recover attorneys' fees and costs is denied. Although Defendant Bembab Behnood's anti-SLAPP motion was poorly conceived and supported, both factually and legally, the Court cannot find that it was frivolous (Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(c)(1)), such that any reasonable attorney would agree that the motion was totally devoid of merit. (See Albanese v. Menounos (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 923, 937 ["Although we rejected [defendant's] contentions on the merits, we cannot say her contentions were frivolous or exceeded the bounds of rational argument."].)

Order is signed.
